title: Yacht Charter Prices in Marbella: 2026 Costs Guide
html:
Yacht charter prices in Marbella in 2026 typically run from around EUR 90 for a shared sunset cruise to EUR 6,000 a day for a fully crewed superyacht, with most boats leaving from Puerto Banús marina. BoatHire24 lists verified options across that whole range so you can match budget to boat.

Marbella sits on the Costa del Sol in southern Spain, where calm summer waters and short hops to nearby coves make it one of the Mediterranean's most rentable coasts. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella or list your own.

Where to set off in Marbella

Most charters leave from a handful of well-known points along this coast.

  • Puerto Banús marina: the glamorous main hub, packed with motor yachts, a short cruise west of Marbella town.
  • Puerto Deportivo de Marbella: the central town marina, handy for smaller motorboats and day trips.
  • Puerto de Cabopino: a quieter eastern marina near the Artola dunes, popular for family outings.
  • La Bajadilla (Puerto Pesquero): Marbella's working and leisure port, used by local operators.
  • Estepona marina: a short hop west, often combined with cruises toward Gibraltar on clear days.

How much does it cost to rent a yacht in Marbella?

Expect roughly EUR 90 per person for a shared trip up to about EUR 6,000 a day for a large crewed yacht in peak summer. What you pay depends on length, engine power and whether fuel and a skipper are included. You can compare live rates for a yacht charter in Marbella across dozens of boats before you commit to dates.

  • Shared catamaran or group tour: from around EUR 35 to EUR 90 per person for a few hours.
  • Small motorboat, half day: from around EUR 250 to EUR 450 in EUR for the boat.
  • Catamaran, full day: from around EUR 900 to EUR 1,800 depending on size and crew.
  • Crewed motor yacht or superyacht: from around EUR 2,500 to EUR 6,000 a day in high season.

Which boat to rent in Marbella?

The right boat depends on group size and budget. These are the most common categories quoted on this coast.

  1. Shared catamaran cruise: best for sociable sunset trips, up to about 40 guests, from EUR 35 per person.
  2. Sport motorboat: best for short hops and swim stops, up to 8 guests, from EUR 250 for a half day.
  3. Sport cruiser: a fast day boat for coves and lunch anchorages, up to 11 guests, from EUR 1,200 a day.
  4. Motor yacht: roomy for a full day afloat, up to 12 guests, from EUR 2,200; you can study one example on the Azimut 58 charter page.
  5. Crewed superyacht: best for special occasions with full service, up to 12 guests, from EUR 4,500 a day.

When is the best season to charter in Marbella?

The high season runs from June to September, when warm, settled days push demand and prices to their yearly peak. May and October bring milder rates and quieter marinas while the sea stays pleasant. Afternoon breezes often turn to the Levante, the easterly wind that can build chop along the coast.

Book morning departures in July and August: the sea is glassiest before midday, and the easterly Levante wind tends to strengthen through the afternoon, so an early start usually means a calmer cruise.

Spain follows the European principle that motor yachts above a certain power or length require a recognised licence, while many smaller boats can be hired with a skipper instead. The operator confirms the exact requirements for each boat at booking. Popular anchorages near Marbella sit within about 3 nautical miles, so even a half-day trip in 2026 gives real cruising time.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 90 per person to EUR 6,000 per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: 3 hours to a full day
  • Guest capacity: 8 to 40 depending on boat
  • Currency: EUR
  • Main departure point: Puerto Banús marina

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Do I need a licence to rent a yacht in Marbella?

Larger and more powerful yachts generally need a recognised licence under Spanish rules, but most boats can be hired with a professional skipper instead, and the operator confirms the precise requirement before booking.

How far in advance should I book?

For July and August weekends it is wise to book several weeks ahead, since the best motor yachts sell out quickly, whereas spring and autumn dates often stay available closer to your trip.

Are fuel and crew included in the price?

It varies by listing, so always check the details: some quotes include fuel, skipper and mooring fees, while others add them separately, with the breakdown shown clearly before you confirm.

How many people can join a charter?

Private motor yachts in Marbella usually carry up to 12 guests at sea, while larger shared catamaran cruises welcome around 40 passengers, so the right boat depends on the size of your group.

Comparing real rates beats guessing, and the BoatHire24 marketplace lets you line up boats, dates and inclusive prices for Marbella before you decide. Owners with a yacht on the Costa del Sol can also list it to earn from quiet weeks, while travellers simply browse, compare and book.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
